text: I find myself surrounded by sleek glass and modern design, a space that feels meticulously planned and somewhat impersonal. The silence here is tangible, punctuated only by the soft hum of the lights and the distant buzz from the other room. I feel like an observer, caught between worlds—outside, anticipation; inside, innovation. The second space is vibrant, filled with ideas and color, inviting curiosity. Though there is no warmth from another presence, the environment itself urges me to ponder the future and my relation to it. I feel contemplative, slightly detached, as if on the threshold of discovery.
